essence: Supports ecological surveys and conservation work by collecting field data on wildlife, habitats, and environmental conditions.

identity.title: Ecology Field Technician
identity.summary: Carries out wildlife surveys, habitat assessments, and environmental monitoring to support ecologists, conservationists, and environmental projects.

salary.notes: Technicians with specialist survey licences (e.g., bats, newts) or working in major consultancies can exceed £45k–£55k+.

qualifications: Training in ecology, environmental science, or conservation
Knowledge of wildlife identification and habitat assessment
Ability to conduct field surveys and collect accurate data
Understanding of ecological regulations and protected species

lifestyle.description: Ecology field technicians work outdoors across woodlands, rivers, wetlands, grasslands, and development sites. They collect ecological data, support environmental assessments, and contribute to conservation projects. The role suits individuals who enjoy fieldwork, wildlife, and hands‑on environmental science.

habits: Uses field equipment such as GPS, traps, and sensors
